title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the engineering backlog (replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay here.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item.

items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new `functions/_lib/auth.js` issues/verifies HMAC-SHA256 tokens. `/api/admin-key` now returns a short-lived signed token (TTL `ADMIN_TOKEN_TTL_SEC`, default 2h; signed with `TOKEN_SECRET`→ falls back to `ADMIN_KEY`) instead of the raw key — the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser. The admin page stores/sends the token (x-admin-key header for writes; `bb_staging` cookie for staging launch). `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the `_middleware` staging gate verify it via `isAdminAuthorized()`; the raw key stays accepted server-side as a fallback (never handed to a browser). Tokens expire. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test (valid/tamper/expiry/fallback). Full E2E needs the deployed Access+KV env.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `/api/admin-key` verifies the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` RS256 signature against the team JWKS (`<ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN>/cdn-cgi/access/certs`, cached 1h) plus audience (`ACCESS_AUD`), issuer, and expiry before issuing a token (`verifyAccessJwt` in `functions/_lib/auth.js`). When `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset it falls back to the prior header-presence check (route is still behind Access + the middleware gate). Verified with a Node WebCrypto test using a generated RSA keypair + mocked JWKS: a genuine token is accepted; forged-signature, absent, expired, wrong-aud, wrong-iss, and unknown-kid are all rejected. Full E2E needs the deployed Access env.

items.title: (staging) Design review: per-day vs per-trade notes & data model
items.doneNote: Design review of how per-DAY and per-TRADE notes fit together. FINDINGS: per-day notes (JOURNAL store, {date,text,updated}) are plain text only, captured in a bare textarea under the calendar; per-trade notes (TRADEMETA store, {id,tags,note,shots,updated}) are rich — tags + note + downscaled screenshots — captured in a modal editor in Manage data. The asymmetry is backwards: the DAY is the natural home for a chart screenshot / morning plan / 'tilted' tag, yet only trades can hold them. Two storage shapes + two capture surfaces mean any future notes feature (search, export, tag filter) must be built twice. The 'note'-only Day-notes list in Manage data is ALREADY fixed — verified in-browser it previews date + full text (newest first); the only bare literal 'note' left is the compact per-trade presence chip (metaChips), which is intentional. RECOMMENDATION: one annotation shape {tags,text,shots}, two scopes (day|trade). Low-risk path = extend the JOURNAL record to {date,text,tags,shots,updated} (old {date,text} records read fine, fields default empty; additive export), reuse the trade editor's tag input + screenshot downscale pipeline, and replace the bare calendar textarea with a shared annotation editor (quick textarea that expands to full editor). Keep the CH11 capture-the-date autosave and the SHOT downscale pipeline. Spun off F16 (unify: give day notes tags + screenshots), CH19 (extract a shared annotation-editor component), CH20 (Manage-data Day-notes rows open/edit + consistent note·img·tag markers). No app code shipped — R3 is review-only.

items.title: Decide what business information the site needs
items.doneNote: Decided what business info the site needs and where it lives. CURRENT STATE: footer + legal.html carry good Disclaimer + Terms-of-Use + a Privacy SUMMARY + contact@blotterbook.com; but the footer has NO copyright line, the operating entity (LICENSE: 'Scaline Audio / Mitchell Guthrie') appears nowhere public, there is no formal Privacy Policy / Terms of Service / refund policy / governing-law clause, and the pricing page already advertises $25/$50 donations + a future paid add-on. OWNER DECISIONS (2026-06): governing law = Delaware; published contact = support email only (contact@blotterbook.com); paid tiers = voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations (no goods exchanged); operating-entity display name = TBD. CHECKLIST / PLACEMENT — Have now: not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers (legal + footer), as-is + liability + proprietary terms (legal), privacy summary (legal), support email (footer/legal). items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Canonical procedure: the README section 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' (the gate-layer model + 8-step checklist) — read it first; this prompt is the operational driver, the README is the source of truth.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request. If none are named, inventory the full staging delta and promote all of it (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Build the inventory by grepping: `STAGING_PAGE` and `if(!STAGING_PAGE)` / `if(STAGING_PAGE)` guards in `app/*.js`; the whole of `app/staging.js`; `<!--IF mode=staging-->` blocks in `partials/*.html`; inline markup in `app/staging.html` that isn't yet in a partial; and the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`. List each feature and its gate layers before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) un-gate JS — move shared logic out of `staging.js` into its module, or delete the `STAGING_PAGE` runtime guard so it runs on every surface. (2) un-gate HTML — move inline staging markup into the right `partials/app-*.html`, or widen its `<!--IF mode=staging-->` to the modes it should reach (normally all three). (3) DEMO 1:1 — the feature MUST appear on demo, single-sourced from the SAME partial (never fork a separate demo copy — that is exactly what drifted before), with every TRADE-mutating control `disabled` in the `<!--IF mode=demo-->` path AND guarded by `DEMO_MODE`; read-only features (activity terminal, session pill) and UI-preference state (saved layouts) just mirror over. (4) if a path is no longer staging-exclusive, update the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`.

THEN: run `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`; verify all three surfaces in a browser (prod: feature works and persists; demo: feature visible with trade-mutations disabled and nothing saved; staging: unchanged); run the gate — `node scripts/test-adapters.cjs`, `test-version.mjs`, `test-auth.mjs`, plus build-includes/build-manifest idempotency. Title the commit/PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level automatically — do NOT hand-edit `data/versions.json`; staging keeps its lead. Add a `data/changelog.json` (F13) entry for the new prod version describing what shipped. Commit + push (open a PR only if asked).

Each run done when: the in-scope staging features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo trade-mutations disabled), all three surfaces verified, the gate is green, a changelog entry is added, and the change is committed/pushed. Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
